goal: Layer CRUD + bi-temporal queries + hot-staging writes on top of the existing §12.1 `knowledge_graph.rs` schema (landed by WO-0011) so that P1-W4-F02 (CRUD + bi-temporal) and P1-W4-F08 (hot staging) flip to passes=true. Supersedes the abandoned WO-0012; fresh branch avoids the stale worktree state documented in `verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md`. Unblocks the downstream Week-4 cascade: P1-W4-F03 (symbol resolution), P1-W4-F04 (extraction pipeline), P1-W4-F10 (get_conventions), and transitively P1-W4-F05 / P1-W4-F09 / P1-W5-F09.
plan_summary: Extend `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs` with typed `Entity` / `Relation` / `HotObservation` structs plus CRUD + bi-temporal + hot-staging helpers. Every writer routes through the existing `execute_in_transaction` chokepoint (which already uses `TransactionBehavior::Immediate`). INIT_SQL stays byte-for-byte frozen — the entities/relations/hot_observations/hot_convention_signals/hot_architecture_deltas tables + their UNIQUE/foreign-key constraints are already in place from WO-0011 (verified at `knowledge_graph.rs:111-216`). All tests live at module root as `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_<name>() { ... }` (DEC-0005 / WO-0006-WO-0007 selector-mismatch avoidance) mirroring the existing `test_schema_creation` at line 472-474. Timestamps use `chrono::DateTime<Utc>::to_rfc3339()` so `t_valid_from`/`t_valid_to` string-comparisons in SQLite are lexicographically correct. The `upsert_entity` flow uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from) DO UPDATE SET t_last_verified = datetime('now'), access_count = access_count + 1 RETURNING id` so the caller gets the inserted-or-updated row id in a single round-trip. `stage_hot_observation` / `stage_hot_convention_signal` / `stage_hot_architecture_delta` writers route through `execute_in_transaction` to satisfy the master-plan §11 line 1117 BEGIN IMMEDIATE invariant. A new `WalCheckpointMode { Passive, Truncate }` enum + `checkpoint_wal` helper wraps `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(<MODE>)` for the configurable interval (F08). Workspace root `Cargo.toml` gains `chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["clock", "serde", "std"] }` under `[workspace.dependencies]` (matches how `uuid` is organised at line 70); `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml` pulls it via `chrono = { workspace = true }`. No schema DDL change. No mocks of `rusqlite::Connection` — every test opens a real tempfile-backed SQLite db via `tempfile::TempDir::new()` + `KnowledgeGraph::open(...)`.
scope_in: Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add `chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["clock", "serde", "std"] }` to `[workspace.dependencies]` (default-features OFF avoids the `oldtime` / `time 0.1` transitive chain that emits clippy-deny warnings on some rustc versions — see RCA §Risk point 5)
crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml — add `chrono = { workspace = true }` under `[dependencies]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct Entity` with fields (exact order): `pub id: Option<i64>` (None before insert, Some(rowid) after), `pub kind: String`, `pub name: String`, `pub qualified_name: Option<String>`, `pub file_path: String`, `pub start_line: Option<i64>`, `pub end_line: Option<i64>`, `pub signature: Option<String>`, `pub doc_comment: Option<String>`, `pub language: Option<String>`, `pub t_valid_from: Option<String>` (RFC-3339), `pub t_valid_to: Option<String>` (RFC-3339), `pub importance: f64`, `pub source_tool: Option<String>`, `pub source_hash: Option<String>` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct Relation` with fields: `pub id: Option<i64>`, `pub source_id: i64`, `pub target_id: i64`, `pub kind: String`, `pub weight: f64`, `pub t_valid_from: Option<String>`, `pub t_valid_to: Option<String>`, `pub source_tool: Option<String>`, `pub source_evidence: Option<String>`, `pub confidence: f64` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct HotObservation` with fields: `pub raw_text: String`, `pub session_id: Option<String>`, `pub related_file: Option<String>`, `pub related_symbol: Option<String>` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `pub enum WalCheckpointMode { Passive, Truncate }` with `impl WalCheckpointMode { const fn as_sql(self) -> &'static str { match self { Self::Passive => "PASSIVE", Self::Truncate => "TRUNCATE" } } }` — derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::upsert_entity(&mut self, entity: &Entity) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; issues `INSERT INTO entities (kind, name, qualified_name, file_path, start_line, end_line, signature, doc_comment, language, t_valid_from, t_valid_to, importance, source_tool, source_hash) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from) DO UPDATE SET t_last_verified = datetime('now'), access_count = access_count + 1 RETURNING id` and returns the resulting rowid. `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self, entity), fields(name=%entity.name))]` per master-plan §15.2 (span name `ucil.core.kg.upsert_entity`)

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::get_entity_by_qualified_name(&self, qualified_name: &str, file_path: Option<&str>) -> Result<Option<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only via `self.conn.prepare(...)?.query_row(...)`; returns the most recent row (`ORDER BY t_ingested_at DESC LIMIT 1`)

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::list_entities_by_file(&self, file_path: &str) -> Result<Vec<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only; returns rows ordered by `start_line` ascending (so callers get document-order)

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::get_entity_as_of(&self, qualified_name: &str, at: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>) -> Result<Option<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — bi-temporal read: `WHERE qualified_name = ?1 AND t_valid_from <= ?2 AND (t_valid_to IS NULL OR t_valid_to > ?2) ORDER BY t_valid_from DESC LIMIT 1`. The `at` parameter is encoded via `at.to_rfc3339()` so the string comparison is lexicographic. `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self))]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::upsert_relation(&mut self, relation: &Relation) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; issues `INSERT INTO relations (source_id, target_id, kind, weight, t_valid_from, t_valid_to, source_tool, source_evidence, confidence) VALUES (...) RETURNING id` (no ON CONFLICT clause — `relations` has no UNIQUE constraint in §12.1)

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::list_relations_by_source(&self, source_id: i64) -> Result<Vec<Relation>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only; returns all rows with the given `source_id`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_observation(&mut self, observation: &HotObservation) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction` (BEGIN IMMEDIATE); issues `INSERT INTO hot_observations (raw_text, session_id, related_file, related_symbol) VALUES (...) RETURNING id`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_convention_signal(&mut self, pattern_hash: &str, file_path: &str, example_snippet: Option<&str>) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; issues `INSERT INTO hot_convention_signals (pattern_hash, file_path, example_snippet) VALUES (...) RETURNING id`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_architecture_delta(&mut self, change_type: &str, file_path: &str, details: Option<&str>) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; issues `INSERT INTO hot_architecture_deltas (change_type, file_path, details) VALUES (...) RETURNING id`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::checkpoint_wal(&self, mode: WalCheckpointMode) -> Result<(i64, i64, i64), KnowledgeGraphError>` — wraps `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(<MODE>)`; returns `(busy, log_frames, checkpointed)` tuple as rusqlite reports it. `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self))]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — extend `KnowledgeGraphError` with the new variants the CRUD paths can surface: `#[error("invalid timestamp: {0}")] InvalidTimestamp(String)` for future use, AND no other new variants (rusqlite errors flow through the existing `#[from] rusqlite::Error` arm). `#[non_exhaustive]` is already on the enum
crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs — extend the existing re-export line so it emits `pub use knowledge_graph::{Entity, HotObservation, KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError, Relation, WalCheckpointMode};`

Module-root tests in `knowledge_graph.rs` — ALL at module level under `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` (NOT inside `mod tests { }`, per DEC-0005 — the `test_schema_creation` precedent at line 472-474 is the shape). Required tests (exact names — `test_hot_staging_writes` is the **frozen F08 selector**):
* `test_upsert_and_get_entity` — inserts an `Entity`, calls `get_entity_by_qualified_name`, asserts all fields round-trip (including `Option<String>` fields)

* `test_bi_temporal_as_of` — inserts three `Entity` rows for the same `qualified_name` with staggered `t_valid_from` and `t_valid_to`; queries `get_entity_as_of` at a timestamp between row-2 and row-3; asserts row-2 is returned; queries at a timestamp after row-3; asserts row-3 is returned
* `test_list_entities_by_file` — inserts 3 `Entity` rows for the same `file_path` with ascending `start_line`; calls `list_entities_by_file`; asserts document-order return
* `test_upsert_relation_and_list` — inserts 2 `Entity` rows, then a `Relation` between them; calls `list_relations_by_source`; asserts the single relation is returned with all fields intact

* `test_entity_unique_constraint_updates` — inserts the same `(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from)` twice; asserts the second call returns the same `id` AND increments `access_count` (read back from the `entities` table) AND updates `t_last_verified`

* `test_hot_staging_writes` — **FROZEN F08 SELECTOR**. Calls `stage_hot_observation`, `stage_hot_convention_signal`, `stage_hot_architecture_delta` each once; reads each table back via `self.conn.query_row("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hot_observations", ...)` (and similar for the other two); asserts each count is 1. Also asserts the call went through a BEGIN IMMEDIATE txn by checking the returned row's id is positive (RETURNING id succeeded)

* `test_wal_checkpoint_truncates` — writes ≥ 10 rows to `hot_observations` to grow the WAL; calls `checkpoint_wal(WalCheckpointMode::Truncate)`; asserts the returned `(busy, log_frames, checkpointed)` tuple has `busy == 0` and `log_frames >= 0` (exact numbers are OS-specific; the point is the call succeeds and returns a tuple)

Rustdoc on every new public item: `Entity`, `Relation`, `HotObservation`, `WalCheckpointMode`, and each new `KnowledgeGraph::*` method — cite master-plan §12.1 (schema), §11 line 1117 (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), §15.2 (tracing spans). Do NOT add new module-level rustdoc — the existing rustdoc block at lines 1-42 already covers the module; extend it only if pedantic requires it.
scope_out: No symbol-resolution UX — P1-W4-F03 is a separate feature. Do NOT implement `resolve_symbol(...)`; `get_entity_by_qualified_name` is the primitive it will use later
No tree-sitter extraction pipeline wiring — P1-W4-F04 is a separate feature; this WO does not call `ucil_treesitter::SymbolExtractor`
No MCP tool plumbing — `find_definition` is P1-W4-F05 (daemon territory); this WO stops at the crate boundary

No changes to `INIT_SQL` / schema DDL — all tables + columns + indexes needed are already in place from WO-0011 (`knowledge_graph.rs:110-305`). If any column looks missing, STOP and write an ADR rather than editing INIT_SQL
No promote-hot-to-warm logic — merge-consolidator is Phase-2+ territory (§12)
No `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper — DEC-0005 / WO-0006 / WO-0007 selector-mismatch regression. Tests live at module root
No mocking of `rusqlite::Connection` — tests open a real tempfile-backed SQLite db (the `test_schema_creation` precedent at `knowledge_graph.rs:499-503`)
No new crate, no crate rename, no new `[workspace.members]` entry

No new ADR — the §12.1 schema, §11 line 1117 BEGIN IMMEDIATE invariant, §9.1 data layout, and DEC-0005 flat-tests rule are all unambiguous for this WO. Raise a proposed ADR ONLY if a genuine spec ambiguity or build failure surfaces
No edits to `ucil-build/feature-list.json` (forbidden by root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract)
No edits to `verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md` — it is the RCA record used as context for this retry; keep it as historical record

No work on `feat/WO-0012-kg-crud-and-hot-staging` — that branch has the uncommitted `Cargo.toml` edit + zero commits; the fresh `feat/WO-0020-kg-crud-and-hot-staging` branch starts clean from `main` to avoid inheriting stale state
acceptance: New CRUD + hot-staging helpers exist in `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs`; `lib.rs` re-exports `Entity, Relation, HotObservation, WalCheckpointMode` alongside the existing `KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError`
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-core 'knowledge_graph::'` matches ≥ 7 tests (6 new F02/F08 tests + the pre-existing `test_schema_creation`), all passing — the F02 acceptance selector (frozen)
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-core knowledge_graph::test_hot_staging_writes` matches exactly 1 test, 1 passed — the F08 acceptance selector (frozen)
`cargo clippy -p ucil-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean (pedantic + nursery — same regime WO-0011 established at `lib.rs:7`)
`cargo doc -p ucil-core --no-deps` is clean (no broken intra-doc links)
Reality-check oracle: `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W4-F02` passes (stashing the new CRUD helpers causes the F02 selector to return < 7 tests OR build failure; popping restores)
Reality-check oracle: `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W4-F08` passes (stashing the hot-staging helpers causes `test_hot_staging_writes` to fail or not resolve; popping restores)
No mocks of `rusqlite::Connection` — tests open a real LMDB-free SQLite db backed by `tempfile::TempDir`
INIT_SQL is byte-for-byte unchanged (`git diff` against `origin/main` shows 0 lines in the DDL string literal region, `knowledge_graph.rs:110-305`)
Module-root tests are flat — no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper anywhere in `knowledge_graph.rs` — per DEC-0005
No new crate, no edit to `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-daemon/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`
`chrono` workspace dep configured with `default-features = false, features = ["clock", "serde", "std"]` (the `oldtime` / `time 0.1` transitive chain is NOT pulled in)
Every writer goes through `execute_in_transaction` — grep for `self.conn.execute(` in the new code returns nothing (reads via `self.conn.prepare(...)?.query_row(...)` / `.query_map(...)` are fine)
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-core 'knowledge_graph::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -E '^\s+PASS' | awk 'END{exit (NR < 7)}'
cargo nextest run -p ucil-core knowledge_graph::test_hot_staging_writes --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -cE '^\s+PASS.*test_hot_staging_writes' | awk '{exit ($1 != 1)}'
context_refs: master-plan:§11 line 1108-1117 (PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL; busy_timeout = 10000; BEGIN IMMEDIATE for every writer)
master-plan:§12.1 lines 1127-1318 (§12.1 schema DDL — the 16 tables + indexes — already landed in INIT_SQL by WO-0011; DO NOT edit)

master-plan:§15.2 (tracing span naming convention: `ucil.<layer>.<op>` — span names `ucil.core.kg.upsert_entity`, `ucil.core.kg.get_entity_as_of`, `ucil.core.kg.stage_hot_observation`, `ucil.core.kg.checkpoint_wal`)
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 4 lines 1749-1754 (CRUD + bi-temporal + hot-staging scope)
master-plan:§9.1 data layout (`.ucil/shared/knowledge.db` — the target path, already realised by `KnowledgeGraph::open`)
decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits (module-root tests — critical here; `test_hot_staging_writes` is the F08 frozen selector)
decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate (acceptance gate doesn't require cargo-mutants; reality-check.sh is the mutation oracle)

work-order:WO-0012-kg-crud-and-hot-staging (ABANDONED PREDECESSOR — same features, rejected for executor session-abandonment, not content. The envelope at `ucil-build/work-orders/0012-kg-crud-and-hot-staging.json` and the RCA at `verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md` contain the full 8-commit remediation plan. WO-0020 supersedes with fresh branch + lessons folded in)
rca:ucil-build/verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md (full remediation plan — commit sequence, non-negotiable invariants, and known pitfalls. The executor MUST read this before starting)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror; pedantic+nursery; tracing; no unwrap outside #[cfg(test)]; no unsafe without ADR; edition 2021)
phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariant 1: no mocks of SQLite; invariant 8: SQLite opens in WAL mode with PRAGMA busy_timeout = 10000 — already enforced in WO-0011)
lessons_applied: Re-using an abandoned WO's plan — WO-0012's envelope + RCA are the source-of-truth for 'what to build'. WO-0020 is a fresh branch (`feat/WO-0020-kg-crud-and-hot-staging`) starting from clean `main` to avoid inheriting the stale uncommitted `Cargo.toml` edit from `feat/WO-0012-*`. Do NOT rebase or cherry-pick from WO-0012's branch — start fresh

Session-abandonment was the sole failure mode of WO-0012 (RCA 95% confidence) — there is NO technical blocker. `cargo build --workspace` was green on the WO-0012 worktree; the spec is unambiguous; the helper `execute_in_transaction` is already in place. The executor discipline that delivered WO-0018 (tag_cache) and WO-0019 (chunker) recently is exactly the pattern to apply here

Module-root flat tests (no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper) per DEC-0005 — the frozen selector `knowledge_graph::` (F02) + `knowledge_graph::test_hot_staging_writes` (F08) are both path prefixes; placing tests flat mirrors `test_schema_creation` at `knowledge_graph.rs:472-474` and the WO-0017/WO-0018/WO-0019 precedent. This was the WO-0006 / WO-0007 regression mode and the RCA §Invariants line 1

INIT_SQL stays byte-for-byte frozen (scope_out item) — all required tables + columns + UNIQUE constraints are already in place. `entities.UNIQUE(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from)` is at line 125, relations columns at 128-137, hot-staging tables at 190-216. Do NOT add columns or indexes. If a column is missing, STOP and write an ADR

`upsert_entity` uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from) DO UPDATE SET t_last_verified = datetime('now'), access_count = access_count + 1 RETURNING id` — the `RETURNING id` gives the caller the row id on both insert AND conflict branches in one round-trip. `test_entity_unique_constraint_updates` will flake if the conflict branch does not return id

`get_entity_as_of` string comparison: `t_valid_from`/`t_valid_to` are TEXT columns in SQLite; pick ONE timestamp format (RFC-3339 via `chrono::DateTime<Utc>::to_rfc3339()`) and use it consistently. Do NOT mix `datetime('now')` (space separator) with RFC-3339 (T separator + offset) on the same column used in range queries

All writers (`upsert_entity`, `upsert_relation`, `stage_hot_observation`, `stage_hot_convention_signal`, `stage_hot_architecture_delta`) MUST route through `execute_in_transaction` (the existing helper at `knowledge_graph.rs:430-440` uses `TransactionBehavior::Immediate`). Direct `self.conn.execute(...)` for writes is forbidden — the grep guard in `acceptance_criteria` will catch it

Reads (`get_entity_by_qualified_name`, `get_entity_as_of`, `list_entities_by_file`, `list_relations_by_source`) use `self.conn.prepare(...)?.query_row(...)` or `.query_map(...)` — no transaction needed

`chrono` workspace dep MUST use `default-features = false, features = ["clock", "serde", "std"]` — default-features pulls the `oldtime` / `time 0.1` transitive chain that emits clippy-deny warnings on some rustc versions (RCA §Risk point 5). Model after `uuid` at `Cargo.toml:70`

Re-exports: `lib.rs` line 22 currently has `pub use knowledge_graph::{KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError};` — extend to `pub use knowledge_graph::{Entity, HotObservation, KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError, Relation, WalCheckpointMode};` (alphabetical so clippy-pedantic `missing_docs_in_private_items` doesn't trip)

No mocks of `rusqlite::Connection` (phase-log invariant 1) — tests open a real tempfile-backed SQLite db via `tempfile::TempDir::new()` + `KnowledgeGraph::open(...)`. The `test_schema_creation` pattern at `knowledge_graph.rs:499-503` is the exemplar

Clippy pedantic + nursery are in effect (inherited from `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs:7`) — expect `doc_markdown`, `missing_const_for_fn`, `missing_errors_doc`, `must_use_candidate`, `option_if_let_else` on the additions. Fix, don't blanket-allow

Conventional Commits with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W4-F02` (or `-F08` on the hot-staging + WAL commits), `Work-order: WO-0020` trailers on every commit; push after every commit; no `--amend` after push; no force-push. The RCA §Commit plan prescribes 8 commits totaling ~490 lines

Session-abandonment prevention — before ending the session verify (a) at least one `.rs` diff against `origin/main` exists on the feature branch, (b) every commit has been pushed, AND (c) `0020-ready-for-review.md` has been written. If any is missing, either keep working or write an escalation explaining the blocker. This is the WO-0012 anti-pattern to avoid

Reality-check / mutation-oracle survival (cf. WO-0017 / WO-0018 / WO-0019 pattern): `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W4-F02` and `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W4-F08` MUST both pass on the delivered branch. Stashing the new CRUD + hot-staging code must cause the selectors to fail (via compile error or missing tests); popping must restore
Do NOT edit `verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md` — it is the RCA record used as context. Keep it as historical record alongside WO-0012's envelope

commit_plan.subject: add chrono workspace dep (default-features off) + wire into ucil-core
knowledge_graph: add Entity + Relation + HotObservation + WalCheckpointMode with serde derives; re-export from lib.rs
knowledge_graph: upsert_entity + get_entity_by_qualified_name + list_entities_by_file + 3 module-root tests
